title: euphrasia rostkoviana subsp. campestris
intro: Euphrasia rostkoviana subsp. campestris, also known as field eye or marsh eye, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant contains bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as soothing eye irritations, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Euphrasia rostkoviana subsp. campestris is often used to make herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various eye and skin conditions.
